## §1. Use Case & Actors
<!-- confidence 1.00 (filled 2/2) -->
- Automated FNOL intake agent for auto claims
- Customer filing an auto claim; human adjuster

## §2. Workflow Ordering
<!-- confidence 1.00 (filled 3+/3) -->
- First classify claim severity, then enrich in parallel from three sources
- Coordinate the enrichment steps and assemble a unified claim record
- Route high-severity claims to a human adjuster before finalizing
- Loop enrichment-and-scoring until completeness score >= 0.9

## §3. Scope, Throughput & Latency
<!-- confidence 0.33 (filled 1/3) -->
- P95 end-to-end intake < 5 minutes

> [NEEDS CLARIFICATION — Epic did not state this; fill in during /specify review]

## §4. Data Sources
<!-- confidence 1.00 (filled 2+/2) -->
- Policy system (transactional, read/write)
- VIN vehicle lookup service
- Weather service (enrichment)
- Claims datastore (write assembled claim)

## §5. External Partners
<!-- confidence 0.00 (filled 0/1) -->
_None stated in the Epic (optional)._

## §6. Actors & Permissions
<!-- confidence 0.00 (filled 0/1) -->

## §7. Business Rules (IF/THEN)
<!-- confidence 1.00 (filled 2+/2) -->
- IF a body shop operates their own estimate system THEN obtain the estimate via that partner
- IF claim-completeness score < 0.9 THEN re-run enrichment (loop)
- IF severity is high THEN route to a human adjuster

## §8. Error Handling & Edge Cases
<!-- confidence 0.00 (filled 0/1) -->
[NEEDS CLARIFICATION — Epic did not state this; fill in during /specify review]

## §9. Non-Functional Requirements
<!-- confidence 1.00 (filled 2+/2) -->
- Availability 99.9%
- RPO 5 min / RTO 30 min
- PII encrypted at rest and in transit

## §10. Acceptance Criteria
<!-- confidence 1.00 (filled 3+/3) -->
- P95 end-to-end < 5 minutes
- Severity classification accuracy > 95%
- 100% of high-severity claims routed to a human adjuster
- No PII in logs
